response:
Let's check if \( 22717 \) is a prime number.

First, note that \( \sqrt{22717} \approx 150.72 \), so we only need to test divisibility by primes up to \( 151 \).

---

**Step 1: Check small primes**

- Not even (last digit 7).
- Sum of digits: \( 2+2+7+1+7 = 19 \), not divisible by 3.
- Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.
- Check 7: \( 22717 \div 7 \):  
  \( 7 \times 3245 = 22715 \), remainder 2, so no.
- Check 11: alternating sum \( 2 - 2 + 7 - 1 + 7 = 13 \), not divisible by 11.
- Check 13: \( 13 \times 1747 = 22711 \), remainder 6, so no.
- Check 17: \( 17 \times 1336 = 22712 \), remainder 5, so no.
- Check 19: \( 19 \times 1195 = 22705 \), remainder 12, so no.
- Check 23: \( 23 \times 987 = 22701 \), remainder 16, so no.
- Check 29: \( 29 \times 783 = 22707 \), remainder 10, so no.
- Check 31: \( 31 \times 733 = 22723 \) (too high), try \( 31 \times 732 = 22692 \), remainder 25, so no.
- Check 37: \( 37 \times 614 = 22718 \) (too high), \( 37 \times 613 = 22681 \), remainder 36, so no.
- Check 41: \( 41 \times III \): \( 41 \times 554 = 22714 \), remainder 3, so no.
- Check 43: \( 43 \times 528 = 22704 \), remainder 13, so no.
- Check 47: \( 47 \times 484 = 22748 \) (too high), \( 47 \times 483 = 22701 \), remainder 16, so no.
- Check 53: \( 53 \times 429 = 22737 \) (too high), \( 53 \times 428 = 22684 \), remainder 33, so no.
- Check 59: \( 59 \times 385 = 22715 \), remainder 2, so no.
- Check 61: \( 61 \times 372 = 22692 \), remainder 25, so no.
- Check 67: \( 67 \times 339 = 22713 \), remainder 4, so no.
- Check 71: \( 71 \times 320 = 22720 \) (too high), \( 71 \times 319 = 22649 \), remainder 68, so no.
- Check 73: \( 73 \times 311 = 22703 \), remainder 14, so no.
- Check 79: \( 79 \times 287 = 22673 \), remainder 44, so no.
- Check 83: \( 83 \times 273 = 22659 \), remainder 58, so no.
- Check 89: \( 89 \times 255 = 22695 \), remainder 22, so no.
- Check 97: \( 97 \times 234 = 22698 \), remainder 19, so no.
- Check 101: \( 101 \times 225 = 22725 \) (too high), \( 101 \times 224 = 22624 \), remainder 93, so no.
- Check 103: \( 103 \times 220 = 22660 \), remainder 57, so no.
- Check 107: \( 107 \times 212 = 22684 \), remainder 33, so no.
- Check 109: \( 109 \times 208 = 22672 \), remainder 45, so no.
- Check 113: \( 113 \times 201 = 22713 \), remainder 4, so no.
- Check 127: \( 127 \times 178 = 22606 \), remainder 111, so no.
- Check 131: \( 131 \times 173 = 22663 \), remainder 54, so no.
- Check 137: \( 137 \times 166 = 22742 \) (too high), \( 137 \times 165 = 22605 \), remainder 112, so no.
- Check 139: \( 139 \times 163 = 22657 \), remainder 60, so no.
- Check 149: \( 149 \times 152 = 22648 \), remainder 69, so no.
- Check 151: \( 151 \times 150 = 22650 \), remainder 67, so no.

---

Since no prime less than or equal to \( 151 \) divides \( 22717 \), it has no divisors other than 1 and itself.

**Therefore, \( 22717 \) is prime.**

\[
\boxed{\text{yes}}
\]
